page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 architecture server within the n4 family, configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel cores with hyper-threading. It features 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a cache hierarchy consisting of 48 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 260 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates average single-core performance but weaker multi-core and memory capabilities. The stress-ng single-core test scored 1577.19, and the Geekbench single-core score reached 1840.0, both representing average tier performance. However, multi-core benchmarks show limitations, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3226.37 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 4108.0, both falling into the weak category. Memory performance is a notable bottleneck; the Passmark memory score is rated as poor at 900.02, and memory bandwidth tests for large blocks show 37.38 GB/sec read and 23.04 GB/sec write speeds.

Despite memory constraints, the CPU supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions. These extensions assist in specific workloads; for instance, LLM inference prompt processing for a small 135M model achieved an average tier speed of 369.4 tokens/second. Static web serving throughput also performed in the average tier at 6.82 GB/second. Qualitatively, the n4-highcpu-4 offers a compute-leaning resource profile with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a cost-efficient option for compute-bound tasks that do not require large memory footprints or high memory bandwidth, though it faces performance trade-offs in memory-intensive or heavily multi-threaded operations. Appropriate workloads include lightweight web serving, single-threaded processing, and small-scale model inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical Intel cores and 8.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, it relies on its Intel processor, which features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks show average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1840.0, but weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth results, including a poor Passmark memory score of 900.02. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides a cost-efficient profile for compute-focused workloads with low memory demands. It is suited for lightweight web serving, basic single-threaded applications, and small-scale machine learning inference using its AMX extensions.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 server with 4 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 8 GB RAM. It features average single-core performance but exhibits weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 server with 4 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and average single-core benchmark performance.
tagline: An x86_64 compute-leaning configuration with dedicated Intel cores, advanced matrix extensions, and a low memory-to-CPU ratio for basic workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set support
Average single-core CPU and static web serving performance
Weak multi-core scaling and poor memory bandwidth scores
Best suited for lightweight web serving and small-scale inference
